summary: Matched the target stage Ganon ground module by replacing asm/melee/gr/grtganon.s with C in src/melee/gr/grtganon.c and adding src/melee/gr/grtganon.h. The build was switched from the assembly object to the C object and configure.py marked grtganon.c as Matching. The implementation reconstructs the stage callback table, StageData, stage initialization, gobj creation/setup, animation callbacks, map-collision callback lookup, and small stub callbacks.
what_changed: Added a 215-line C implementation for src/melee/gr/grtganon.c.
Added src/melee/gr/grtganon.h with prototypes for grTGanon_802246D8 through grTGanon_80224A4C and forward includes for HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, Vec3, and lb_UnkAnimStruct.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grtganon.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grtganon.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grtganon.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grtganon.s.o.
Adjusted src/melee/gr/grtgamewatch.h by removing the unused parameter name from grTGameWatch_802243E4(int unused), leaving grTGameWatch_802243E4(int).
Recreated grTGn_803E9880 as a StageCallbacks array, grTGn_803E98DC as StageData with stage id 65 and asset path "/GrTGn.dat", and grTGn_804D6B18 as a special-params pointer.

Implemented stage startup logic that stores Ground_801C49F8() in grTGn_804D6B18, edits stage_info.unk8C.b4/b5, creates three ground gobjs via grTGanon_80224784(0), (1), and (2), and calls standard Ground_801C39C0/3BB4/4210/42AC setup routines.

Implemented grTGanon_80224784 as the common gobj setup helper using Ground_801C14D0, HSD_GObjGetUserData, GObj_SetupGXLink, optional StageCallbacks callback0/callback2/callback3 handling, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and an OSReport failure path.
Implemented grTGanon_802249B4 as a map-collision/special-params lookup using mpLib_80056B6C and mpLib_80054C6C, returning x0/x4/x8 for bit values 1 << 1, 1 << 2, and 1 << 3 respectively.
smart_moves: Translated the raw .data table into typed StageCallbacks and StageData initializers, making the original function-pointer layout searchable and reusable by later stage decomp work.
Introduced a small grTGnSpecialParams struct with x0/x4/x8 lb_UnkAnimStruct pointers to model the three special-parameter loads previously done through raw offsets 0, 4, and 8 from grTGn_804D6B18.
Used stage_info.unk8C.b4 and b5 bitfields to express the rlwimi byte manipulation in initialization, preserving behavior while improving readability.
Kept the original OSReport string and source line value in the gobj setup failure branch, which is often necessary for matching string pools and immediate constants.
Used the existing GET_GROUND(gobj) helper in animation/setup callbacks while still matching functions that access gobj user data.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in callbacks that call Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138, indicating a stack-shaping tactic needed for matching those functions.
Completed the integration cleanly by updating both configure.py matching status and obj_files.mk object selection.
decomp_lessons: For target-stage modules, a successful asm-to-C match may require reconstructing both code and data: StageCallbacks arrays, StageData metadata, asset path strings, and sbss globals all move from .s into C.

When matching stage gobj creation helpers, preserve the exact callback ordering: clear Ground x8/xC callbacks, setup GX link, optionally assign x1C_callback from callback3, call callback0, then register callback2 as an HSD_GObj proc.
Small do-nothing and constant-return callbacks should still be declared and implemented because they are referenced by StageData and StageCallbacks tables.
Bitfield names on shared structs such as stage_info.unk8C can replace hand-written rlwimi assembly patterns and are important for readable matched C.
Map or collision lookup helpers may match more cleanly when sentinel values are kept explicit, as in checking arg0 != -1 and mpLib_80056B6C(arg0) != -1 before calling mpLib_80054C6C.
Header hygiene matters during matching: prototypes should use established project types and forward headers rather than pulling in full definitions when only pointers are needed.
Evidence from this PR is mostly the diff; there were no PR body notes or review comments explaining author intent.
naming_conventions: Functions use the module prefix grTGanon_ followed by the original address, for example grTGanon_80224784 and grTGanon_802249B4.
Data globals use the shortened stage prefix grTGn_, for example grTGn_803E9880, grTGn_803E98DC, and grTGn_804D6B18.
The stage asset path is preserved as the exact string "/GrTGn.dat".
The local special-parameter struct is named grTGnSpecialParams and keeps offset-style fields x0, x4, and x8 because their semantic names were not established in the PR.
The new header uses the guard _grtganon_h_.
Nearby header cleanup removed an unused parameter name from grTGameWatch_802243E4, suggesting bare parameter types are acceptable for unnamed or unused prototype parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a whole assembly file with C and switched the build from asm/melee/gr/grtganon.s.o to src/melee/gr/grtganon.c.o.
Marked the object Matching in configure.py only after the C implementation was in place.
Preserved callback-table ordering and NULL entries from the assembly .data section in a typed StageCallbacks array.
Preserved StageData constants including stage id 65, flags (1 << 0), and callback function slots.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in grTGanon_802248A8 and grTGanon_80224938 to shape the stack for matching.
Preserved exact OSReport format text "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", filename "grtganon.c", and line constant 201.
Expressed assembly comparisons against constants 2, 4, and 8 as bit-shift forms (1 << 1), (1 << 2), and (1 << 3).
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Check whether src/melee/gr/grtganon.h intentionally contains duplicate prototypes for grTGanon_802248A8.
Compare grtganon.c against nearby target-stage modules such as grtgamewatch.c and grtemblem.c for shared StageCallbacks and StageData patterns.
Look for later PRs that rename grTGnSpecialParams fields x0/x4/x8 or identify the semantics of the returned lb_UnkAnimStruct pointers.
Search for other uses of stage_info.unk8C.b4 and stage_info.unk8C.b5 to clarify the meaning of the initialization flags.
Search for other target-stage OSReport strings with "couldn t get gobj" to reuse matching line-number/string-pool tactics.
